title: Campus-Public Visibility Drives Higher Engagement
body: Events with campus-wide visibility show a 14.8% higher RSVP positive rate and 13.6% higher attendance compared to friends-only events. This effect is statistically significant (p=0.0000).
recommendation: Expand campus-public visibility as a default for eligible event types. Consider A/B testing opt-in campus visibility for hosts.

title: Campus Visibility Most Effective for Study Group Events
body: Study Group events see the largest RSVP lift from campus visibility (15.8%), while Cultural events see the smallest (14.4%).
recommendation: Prioritize campus visibility promotion for Study Group and Sports Watch event types where the feature has the most impact.

title: 73 Universities Identified as High-Growth Hubs
body: These campuses show 211 events/1K users (vs cluster average), with 4% host repeat rate and 32% RSVP yes rate.
recommendation: Study these campuses for growth playbook insights. Consider dedicated campus ambassadors for sustained momentum.

title: Power Hosts (7% of users) Drive Disproportionate Value
body: Just 7% of users host 0.1 events on average. They also attend 2.2 events and have 48 friends.
recommendation: Protect and nurture power hosts with hosting tools, analytics, and recognition. Their churn would have outsized platform impact.

title: Campus Visibility Improves 4-Week Retention
body: Users whose first event had campus visibility show 12.0% 4-week retention vs 12.0% for friends-only first events — a 0.0pp difference.
recommendation: Default new events to campus-visible for first-time hosts to maximize early retention lift.

title: Event Volume Varies 6x Between Peak and Trough Weeks
body: Peak activity: Week 0 (5,558 events). Lowest: Week 21 (478 events). This ~12x variation follows academic calendar patterns.
recommendation: Pre-load engagement campaigns before semester starts. Reduce marketing spend during break periods. Use trough weeks for platform maintenance.

title: Wide Variance in Campus Engagement Density
body: Top 5 campuses by event density generate 263+ events/1K users, while bottom 5 generate under 183/1K. The top performer is Virginia Tech.
recommendation: Investigate what makes top-performing campuses successful and replicate those conditions elsewhere.
